hintDisplay: Sujeto: ellos / Verbo: pronosticar (predicción enfática con inversión) / Complemento: lluvia torrencial para mañana / Tiempo: mañana
spanishSentence: Nunca han pronosticado ellos lluvia tan torrencial para mañana
correctSentence: Never have they forecasted such torrential rain for tomorrow

correctSentence: Never have I flooded the basement with water during the storm.

correctSentence: Never have I been so frozen during the polar night.

correctSentence: Never had I seen it snow so intensely during the night

hintDisplay: Sujeto: ellos / Verbo: calentarse / Complemento: considerablemente durante el verano / Tiempo: durante el verano

correctSentence: Rarely do they warm up so considerably during the summer as they are doing this year.

correctSentence: Never has the forecast improved in the coming days.

correctSentence: Never has the forecast predicted rain for tomorrow.

correctSentence: Not only have they not cooled down the temperature during the night this week, but it has increased.

correctSentence: Never has the forecast improved so much during tomorrow's afternoon

correctSentence: Never have we cooled down so considerably during the night as in this winter

correctSentence: Never has the atmosphere warmed so rapidly before a storm.
